sources.paper: A Practical Sublinear Approximation for Group Steiner Tree
sources.paper_status: accepted by VLDB 2026; not public at the 2026-07-23 freeze
sources.source_status: private author-supplied processed graph/query interfaces; manuscript/source material is not Git-tracked
sources.public_precursor_caveat: useful provenance context only; it is not asserted to be byte-identical to the five final MonoGST+ inputs
sources.identity_confidence: high for the local frozen interfaces because final SHA-256 values are recorded; public independent acquisition remains unresolved
sources.redistribution: blocking before public artifact: obtain author permission or publish author-approved acquisition instructions and hashes

sources.paper: Fast Optimal Group Steiner Tree Search using GPUs
sources.graph_policy: exact processed author .in and .g products converted without changing graph semantics
sources.query_policy: all first 300 author CSV rows at each of g=3,5,7
sources.identity_confidence: high for author-product identity: OneDrive metadata, source hashes and converted-interface hashes are recorded
sources.redistribution: repository/data-package redistribution terms are not explicit; publish acquisition instructions and hashes unless permission is obtained

sources.paper_protocol: related-group co-occurrence BFS from Approximating Probabilistic Group Steiner Trees in Graphs
sources.selection: five log1p(realized mean group-size) rank strata; one stable-hash query per stratum
sources.claim_boundary: method-faithful newly generated extension with new seeds and input-only panel selection; not a recovery of any published query sample

sources.graph_transform: one title or person per vertex; undirected title-person principal edges with positive unit weight; dense 1-based IDs
sources.claim_boundary: dated official sensitivity graph, not the unavailable PrunedDP++ 2016 IMDb snapshot

sources.protocol: MonoGST+: independent rounded N(f,(0.15f)^2) sizes clamped to [0.5f,1.5f], then uniform vertices without replacement within each group
sources.adaptation: retain the supplied sampling rule while changing the preregistered g/f grid and using five rather than ten queries per cell
